growth_records.strain: Enrichment culture A (tidal wetland soil-derived, CO2/H2-grown, C. thailandense-dominated)
growth_records.medium: Modified PETC-MES medium + 1.5 g/L yeast extract, CO2/H2 gas feed
growth_records.cite: Rodriguez et al. Abundant and active acetogens enhance the carbon dioxide sink of Blue Carbon ecosystems. Microbiome (2025). DOI:10.1186/s40168-025-02209-4. [PMC12529827]
growth_records.snippet: Both cultures started consuming CO2 and H2 after ~24 h from inoculation in parallel to exponential growth. During this growth phase, cultures A and B exhibited growth rates of 0.039 and 0.029 h-1.
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate (mu_max = 0.039 h-1) directly reported for enrichment culture A, calculated by the authors from 3 exponential-phase OD600 data points (R2>0.99) in a batch bioreactor (modified PETC-MES + 1.5 g/L yeast extract, continuous CO2/H2 gas flow 8 mL/min, pH 5.5 maintained with NaOH, 37°C, anaerobic). Culture is a soil-derived enrichment dominated by C. thailandense (not an axenic isolate); doubling_time_h = ln2/0.039 = 17.77 h, computed here from the reported mu. Volumetric gas consumption rates (mmol/L/day) and acetate/ethanol/formate titers (e.g., 64 mM acetate in culture A at stationary phase) are mentioned but only shown graphically (Fig. 4) without numeric values in the main text, so uptake/secretion rates could not be extracted.
